document.name: microservice-dev-session
document.title: Microservice dev-session: provision N live dev previews (multi-service), then hand off to an interactive coding agent
document.summary: Generalizes the per-run live-sync loop to N microservices at once (workflow-builder/workflow-orchestrator/function-router). Provisions each service's per-run dev preview (kept alive, browsable at the preview URL), clones the repo once into the shared /sandbox/work + writes a fan-out sync.sh (per-service .syncenv.d entries), then ENDS INTO a persistent interactive coding-agent session bound to the same workspace + previews. `services` is a CSV (default "workflow-builder"); the fan-out is opt-in by listing more than one. B1 of the agentic dev loop.

services.title: Microservice(s) to develop (CSV)
services.description: Comma-separated logical services from the dev-preview registry (workflow-builder | workflow-orchestrator | function-router). One = single-service; multiple = simultaneous multi-service adopt in ONE preview.

mode.title: Dev-preview mode
mode.description: preview-native (run INSIDE a Tier-2 vcluster preview: REPLACE each prod pod with its dev server + adopt its Service) | host-throwaway (legacy per-run preview on the host dev cluster).

keepPreview.title: Keep the preview alive after the run

artifacts.from: ${ { ok: (.data.ok // false), services: ([ .data.services[]? | { service: .service, ok: .ok, url: (.info.url // ""), syncUrl: (.info.syncUrl // ""), sandboxName: (.info.sandboxName // ""), ready: (.info.ready // false), error: (.error // "") } ]) } }
artifacts.title: Per-service dev previews

with.command:
${ "export REPOURL=" + ((.trigger.repoUrl // "PittampalliOrg/workflow-builder") | @sh) + "
" + "set -e
cd /sandbox/work
rm -rf repo .syncenv.d
" + "git clone --depth 1 --single-branch https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/$REPOURL.git repo
" + "mkdir -p /sandbox/work/.syncenv.d
" + ( [ .provision_preview.services[]       | select((.info.syncUrl // "") != "")       | "printf 'SUBDIR=%s\nPATHS="%s"\nSYNCURL=%s\nURL=%s\n' "         + ((.info.repoSubdir // ".") | @sh) + " "         + (((.info.syncPaths // ["src"]) | join(" ")) | @sh) + " "         + ((.info.syncUrl) | @sh) + " "         + ((.info.url // "") | @sh)         + " > /sandbox/work/.syncenv.d/" + .service ]     | join("
") ) + "
" + "cat > /sandbox/work/sync.sh <<'SH'
" + "#!/bin/sh
set -e
SVCS="$*"; [ -z "$SVCS" ] && SVCS=$(ls /sandbox/work/.syncenv.d)
" + "for svc in $SVCS; do
" + "  . /sandbox/work/.syncenv.d/$svc
" + "  cd /sandbox/work/repo/$SUBDIR
" + "  SYNCPATHS=""; for p in $PATHS; do [ -e "$p" ] && SYNCPATHS="$SYNCPATHS $p"; done
" + "  tar -czf /tmp/sync-$svc.tgz $SYNCPATHS
" + "  curl -sS -X POST --data-binary @/tmp/sync-$svc.tgz -H 'content-type: application/gzip' "$SYNCURL"
" + "  echo; echo "SYNCED $svc"; cd /sandbox/work
" + "done
SH
" + "chmod +x /sandbox/work/sync.sh
" + "echo CLONED $(git -C repo rev-parse --short HEAD); echo '--- .syncenv.d ---'; for f in /sandbox/work/.syncenv.d/*; do echo "== $f =="; cat "$f"; done" }

with.instructions:
${ "You are an interactive developer for these microservices: **" + ([.provision_preview.services[] | select(.ok) | .service] | join(", ")) + "**.

" + "One repo is cloned at /sandbox/work/repo. Each service has its own subdir + sync target:

" + "| service | edit under | sync | verify (in-cluster) |
|---|---|---|---|
" + ( [ .provision_preview.services[] | select(.ok)       | "| " + .service       + " | repo/" + (.info.repoSubdir // ".")       + " | ./sync.sh " + .service       + " | curl " + (.info.url // "(pending)")       + " |" ] | join("
") ) + "

- Edit files under each service's subdir, then run `./sync.sh` (all services) or `./sync.sh <service>` (one). It hot-reloads in a few seconds.
" + "- VERIFY from inside this sandbox by curling that service's in-cluster URL above. Do NOT curl the public browse URL from here (not resolvable in-cluster).

" + "Reply 'ready' and wait for the user's next message describing the change." }
